content: 1 in 7 Americans live in a pharmacy desert. When your nearest pharmacy is 10+ miles away, knowing exactly what medications you have and when you need refills isn't optional. https://trackmeplus.com
Pharmacies are closing across the country. If getting a refill means a 30-minute drive, you can't afford to realize you're out at the last minute. Track your supply. https://trackmeplus.com
MIT built a pill that texts your doctor when you swallow it. Until that exists for everyone, there's a simpler way to track what you take: log it yourself. https://trackmeplus.com

Managing a loved one's medications on top of your own life is a lot. We wrote a guide for caregivers who need to stay organized. https://docs.trackmeplus.com/2026/03/17/caregivers-guide-managing-medications.html
Nobody teaches you how to manage medications for someone else. You just figure it out. TrackMe+ makes it easier with shared tracking and supply alerts. https://trackmeplus.com
Tracking medications for a parent or partner? You need your own dashboard, not a shared login. TrackMe+ Family lets up to 4 people track independently. https://trackmeplus.com
Remembering your own medications is one thing. Keeping track of someone else's on top of that is a full-time mental job. There's a better way than sticky notes. https://trackmeplus.com
53 million Americans are caregivers. If you're one of them and you're managing medications for someone, you deserve a tool that actually helps. https://trackmeplus.com
